patientHistory:
PATIENT MEDICAL RECORD
Patient ID: MRN-45892
Name: John Anderson
DOB: 03/15/1978 (Age: 45)
Gender: Male
Blood Type: O+

MEDICAL HISTORY SUMMARY:

2015 - INITIAL PRESENTATION
- Chief Complaint: Routine physical examination
- Vital Signs: BP 138/88, HR 78, Temp 98.6°F
- Diagnosed with Stage 1 Hypertension
- Prescribed: Lisinopril 10mg daily
- Lifestyle modifications recommended: diet, exercise
- Family History: Father had MI at age 62, Mother has Type 2 Diabetes

2016 - FOLLOW-UP VISITS
January 2016:
- BP improved to 128/82 on medication
- Weight: 195 lbs, BMI 28.5 (overweight)
- Lipid Panel: Total Cholesterol 245, LDL 165, HDL 38, Triglycerides 210
- Started Atorvastatin 20mg for hyperlipidemia
- Advised smoking cessation (1 pack/day for 20 years)

June 2016:
- Complained of occasional chest discomfort with exertion
- EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, no acute changes
- Stress test ordered - Result: Negative for ischemia
- Continued current medications

2017 - DIABETES DIAGNOSIS
March 2017:
- Fasting glucose: 156 mg/dL, HbA1c: 7.2%
- Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Started Metformin 500mg twice daily
- Referred to diabetes education program
- Dietary consultation completed

September 2017:
- HbA1c improved to 6.8%
- Weight: 188 lbs (lost 7 lbs)
- BP: 125/80, well controlled
- Lipids improving: LDL 142, HDL 42

2018 - CARDIAC WORKUP
February 2018:
- Presented with increased chest discomfort
- Described as pressure-like, substernal, radiating to left arm
- Occurs with moderate exertion, relieved by rest
- Duration: 5-10 minutes per episode
- Frequency: 2-3 times per week

Diagnostic Tests Performed:
- EKG: T-wave flattening in leads V4-V6
- Echocardiogram: Mild LV hypertrophy, EF 55%, no wall motion abnormalities
- Nuclear stress test: Small area of reversible ischemia in LAD territory
- Coronary angiography: 60% stenosis in mid-LAD, 40% stenosis in RCA

Treatment Plan:
- Medical management chosen over intervention
- Added Metoprolol 50mg twice daily
- Added Aspirin 81mg daily
- Added Nitroglycerin SL PRN for chest pain
- Increased Atorvastatin to 40mg
- Cardiac rehabilitation program enrollment

2019 - STABLE ANGINA MANAGEMENT
Quarterly follow-ups throughout 2019:
- Angina episodes reduced to 1-2 per month
- Medications well tolerated
- Weight: 182 lbs (continued weight loss)
- BP: 118/76, HR: 62 (on beta blocker)
- HbA1c: 6.5%, glucose control improved
- Lipids: Total 180, LDL 98, HDL 48, Triglycerides 170
- Successfully quit smoking (6 months smoke-free)

2020 - COVID-19 PANDEMIC
March 2020:
- Telehealth visits initiated due to pandemic
- Medications refilled, no changes
- Patient reports increased stress, working from home
- Exercise routine disrupted

August 2020:
- Tested positive for COVID-19
- Mild symptoms: fever, cough, fatigue
- Managed at home with supportive care
- Full recovery after 2 weeks
- No cardiac complications noted

2021 - POST-COVID MONITORING
January 2021:
- Post-COVID follow-up
- Complained of increased fatigue
- Repeat echocardiogram: EF 52%, mild decrease from baseline
- Troponin: Normal
- BNP: Mildly elevated at 125 pg/mL
- Continued current cardiac medications

June 2021:
- Fatigue improved
- Repeat echo: EF back to 55%
- Exercise tolerance improving
- Weight: 178 lbs
- BP: 120/78, HR: 64

2022 - MEDICATION ADJUSTMENTS
February 2022:
- Routine labs: Creatinine slightly elevated at 1.3 (baseline 1.0)
- eGFR: 68 mL/min (mild CKD Stage 2)
- Lisinopril dose reduced to 5mg due to kidney function
- Metformin continued (safe at this eGFR level)

July 2022:
- Complained of occasional palpitations
- 24-hour Holter monitor: Rare PVCs, no sustained arrhythmias
- Electrolytes: Normal
- Thyroid function: Normal
- Reassured, no treatment needed

November 2022:
- Annual cardiac stress test: Negative for new ischemia
- Coronary CTA: Stable CAD, no progression
- LAD stenosis unchanged at 60%
- No new lesions identified

2023 - RECENT HISTORY
January 2023:
- Routine follow-up
- All medications continued
- Weight: 175 lbs (BMI 25.5 - normal range achieved)
- BP: 122/80, HR: 66
- HbA1c: 6.3% (excellent control)
- Lipids: LDL 85, HDL 52 (at goal)

April 2023:
- Presented with chest pain episode lasting 20 minutes
- Pain resolved spontaneously before arrival
- EKG: No acute changes
- Troponin: Negative x2 (0 and 3 hours)
- Observation overnight, stress test next day: Negative
- Diagnosed as unstable angina vs GERD
- Added Omeprazole 20mg daily
- Increased Metoprolol to 75mg twice daily

August 2023:
- Doing well on current regimen
- Chest pain episodes rare (1 per month)
- Usually related to heavy meals or stress
- Responds well to nitroglycerin when needed
- Exercise tolerance good - walks 2 miles daily

CURRENT MEDICATIONS (as of December 2023):
1. Lisinopril 5mg daily (hypertension, cardioprotection)
2. Metoprolol 75mg twice daily (angina, hypertension)
3. Atorvastatin 40mg nightly (hyperlipidemia)
4. Aspirin 81mg daily (antiplatelet)
5. Metformin 500mg twice daily (diabetes)
6. Omeprazole 20mg daily (GERD)
7. Nitroglycerin 0.4mg SL PRN (angina)

ALLERGIES:
- Penicillin (rash)
- Sulfa drugs (hives)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Occupation: Software engineer (sedentary)
- Marital Status: Married, 2 children
- Alcohol: Occasional (1-2 drinks per week)
- Tobacco: Former smoker, quit 2019
- Exercise: Walks daily, light weights 3x/week
- Diet: Following cardiac diet, reduced sodium

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father: MI age 62, CABG age 65, died age 72 (heart failure)
- Mother: Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, alive age 73
- Brother: Hyperlipidemia, age 48
- Sister: Healthy, age 42

RECENT LAB VALUES (November 2023):
- CBC: WBC 7.2, Hgb 14.5, Plt 245 (normal)
- BMP: Na 140, K 4.2, Cl 102, CO2 26, BUN 18, Cr 1.2, Glucose 108
- Lipids: Total 175, LDL 85, HDL 52, Triglycerides 145
- HbA1c: 6.3%
- TSH: 2.1 (normal)
- Liver enzymes: Normal

IMAGING STUDIES ON FILE:
- Chest X-ray (2023): Clear lungs, normal cardiac silhouette
- Echocardiogram (2023): EF 55%, mild LVH, no valvular disease
- Coronary CTA (2022): Known CAD, stable
- Carotid ultrasound (2021): No significant stenosis

PROCEDURES:
- Coronary angiography (2018): 60% LAD stenosis, medical management
- Multiple stress tests (2016-2023): Most recent negative

HOSPITALIZATIONS:
- None for cardiac reasons
- COVID-19 (2020): Managed at home

EMERGENCY DEPARTMENT VISITS:
- April 2023: Chest pain, ruled out MI, discharged
- March 2018: Chest pain evaluation, admitted for cardiac cath

RISK FACTORS:
- Known coronary artery disease
- Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Hypertension (controlled)
- Hyperlipidemia (controlled)
- Former smoker
- Family history of premature CAD
- Mild chronic kidney disease
- Overweight (improved)

FUNCTIONAL STATUS:
- Independent in all ADLs
- Works full-time
- Exercises regularly
- No limitations in daily activities
- Occasional angina with heavy exertion
emergencyDescription:
Patient presenting to ED via EMS with acute onset severe chest pain. Pain started 45 minutes ago while shoveling snow. Described as crushing, substernal pressure 9/10 severity, radiating to left arm and jaw. Associated with profuse diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. Pain not relieved by 3 doses of home nitroglycerin. Patient appears anxious and in distress. Skin cool, clammy. EMS administered aspirin 324mg and oxygen 4L NC en route.

VITAL SIGNS ON ARRIVAL:
BP: 168/95 mmHg
HR: 102 bpm (regular)
RR: 22 breaths/min
SpO2: 94% on 4L O2
Temp: 98.2°F

PHYSICAL EXAM:
- General: Anxious, diaphoretic, in moderate distress
- Cardiovascular: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs
- Respiratory: Mild tachypnea, clear lung sounds bilaterally
- Extremities: Cool, clammy, pulses intact

IMMEDIATE CONCERNS:
- Acute coronary syndrome (STEMI vs NSTEMI)
- Known CAD with 60% LAD stenosis
- Multiple cardiac risk factors
- Prolonged chest pain unresponsive to nitroglycerin
- Hemodynamically stable but symptomatic

emergencyDescription:
Patient presenting to ED via EMS with acute onset severe chest pain. Pain started 45 minutes ago while shoveling snow. Described as crushing, substernal pressure 9/10 severity, radiating to left arm and jaw. Associated with profuse diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. Pain not relieved by 3 doses of home nitroglycerin. Patient appears anxious and in distress. Skin cool, clammy. EMS administered aspirin 324mg and oxygen 4L NC en route.

VITAL SIGNS ON ARRIVAL:
BP: 168/95 mmHg
HR: 102 bpm (regular)
RR: 22 breaths/min
SpO2: 94% on 4L O2
Temp: 98.2°F

PHYSICAL EXAM:
- General: Anxious, diaphoretic, in moderate distress
- Cardiovascular: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs
- Respiratory: Mild tachypnea, clear lung sounds bilaterally
- Extremities: Cool, clammy, pulses intact

IMMEDIATE CONCERNS:
- Acute coronary syndrome (STEMI vs NSTEMI)
- Known CAD with 60% LAD stenosis
- Multiple cardiac risk factors
- Prolonged chest pain unresponsive to nitroglycerin
- Hemodynamically stable but symptomatic

emergencyDescription:
Patient presenting to ED via EMS with acute onset severe chest pain. Pain started 45 minutes ago while shoveling snow. Described as crushing, substernal pressure 9/10 severity, radiating to left arm and jaw. Associated with profuse diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. Pain not relieved by 3 doses of home nitroglycerin. Patient appears anxious and in distress. Skin cool, clammy. EMS administered aspirin 324mg and oxygen 4L NC en route.

VITAL SIGNS ON ARRIVAL:
BP: 168/95 mmHg
HR: 102 bpm (regular)
RR: 22 breaths/min
SpO2: 94% on 4L O2
Temp: 98.2°F

PHYSICAL EXAM:
- General: Anxious, diaphoretic, in moderate distress
- Cardiovascular: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs
- Respiratory: Mild tachypnea, clear lung sounds bilaterally
- Extremities: Cool, clammy, pulses intact

IMMEDIATE CONCERNS:
- Acute coronary syndrome (STEMI vs NSTEMI)
- Known CAD with 60% LAD stenosis
- Multiple cardiac risk factors
- Prolonged chest pain unresponsive to nitroglycerin
- Hemodynamically stable but symptomatic

emergencyDescription:
Patient presenting to ED via EMS with acute onset severe chest pain. Pain started 45 minutes ago while shoveling snow. Described as crushing, substernal pressure 9/10 severity, radiating to left arm and jaw. Associated with profuse diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. Pain not relieved by 3 doses of home nitroglycerin. Patient appears anxious and in distress. Skin cool, clammy. EMS administered aspirin 324mg and oxygen 4L NC en route.

VITAL SIGNS ON ARRIVAL:
BP: 168/95 mmHg
HR: 102 bpm (regular)
RR: 22 breaths/min
SpO2: 94% on 4L O2
Temp: 98.2°F

PHYSICAL EXAM:
- General: Anxious, diaphoretic, in moderate distress
- Cardiovascular: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs
- Respiratory: Mild tachypnea, clear lung sounds bilaterally
- Extremities: Cool, clammy, pulses intact

IMMEDIATE CONCERNS:
- Acute coronary syndrome (STEMI vs NSTEMI)
- Known CAD with 60% LAD stenosis
- Multiple cardiac risk factors
- Prolonged chest pain unresponsive to nitroglycerin
- Hemodynamically stable but symptomatic

Name: John Anderson
DOB: 03/15/1978 (Age: 45)
Gender: Male
Blood Type: O+

MEDICAL HISTORY SUMMARY:

2015 - INITIAL PRESENTATION
- Chief Complaint: Routine physical examination
- Vital Signs: BP 138/88, HR 78, Temp 98.6°F
- Diagnosed with Stage 1 Hypertension
- Prescribed: Lisinopril 10mg daily
- Lifestyle modifications recommended: diet, exercise
- Family History: Father had MI at age 62, Mother has Type 2 Diabetes

2016 - FOLLOW-UP VISITS
January 2016:
- BP improved to 128/82 on medication
- Weight: 195 lbs, BMI 28.5 (overweight)
- Lipid Panel: Total Cholesterol 245, LDL 165, HDL 38, Triglycerides 210
- Started Atorvastatin 20mg for hyperlipidemia
- Advised smoking cessation (1 pack/day for 20 years)

June 2016:
- Complained of occasional chest discomfort with exertion
- EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, no acute changes
- Stress test ordered - Result: Negative for ischemia
- Continued current medications

2017 - DIABETES DIAGNOSIS
March 2017:
- Fasting glucose: 156 mg/dL, HbA1c: 7.2%
- Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Started Metformin 500mg twice daily
- Referred to diabetes education program
- Dietary consultation completed

September 2017:
- HbA1c improved to 6.8%
- Weight: 188 lbs (lost 7 lbs)
- BP: 125/80, well controlled
- Lipids improving: LDL 142, HDL 42

2018 - CARDIAC WORKUP
February 2018:
- Presented with increased chest discomfort
- Described as pressure-like, substernal, radiating to left arm
- Occurs with moderate exertion, relieved by rest
- Duration: 5-10 minutes per episode
- Frequency: 2-3 times per week

Diagnostic Tests Performed:
- EKG: T-wave flattening in leads V4-V6
- Echocardiogram: Mild LV hypertrophy, EF 55%, no wall motion abnormalities
- Nuclear stress test: Small area of reversible ischemia in LAD territory
- Coronary angiography: 60% stenosis in mid-LAD, 40% stenosis in RCA

Treatment Plan:
- Medical management chosen over intervention
- Added Metoprolol 50mg twice daily
- Added Aspirin 81mg daily
- Added Nitroglycerin SL PRN for chest pain
- Increased Atorvastatin to 40mg
- Cardiac rehabilitation program enrollment

2019 - STABLE ANGINA MANAGEMENT
Quarterly follow-ups throughout 2019:
- Angina episodes reduced to 1-2 per month
- Medications well tolerated
- Weight: 182 lbs (continued weight loss)
- BP: 118/76, HR: 62 (on beta blocker)
- HbA1c: 6.5%, glucose control improved
- Lipids: Total 180, LDL 98, HDL 48, Triglycerides 170
- Successfully quit smoking (6 months smoke-free)

2020 - COVID-19 PANDEMIC
March 2020:
- Telehealth visits initiated due to pandemic
- Medications refilled, no changes
- Patient reports increased stress, working from home
- Exercise routine disrupted

August 2020:
- Tested positive for COVID-19
- Mild symptoms: fever, cough, fatigue
- Managed at home with supportive care
- Full recovery after 2 weeks
- No cardiac complications noted

2021 - POST-COVID MONITORING
January 2021:
- Post-COVID follow-up
- Complained of increased fatigue
- Repeat echocardiogram: EF 52%, mild decrease from baseline
- Troponin: Normal
- BNP: Mildly elevated at 125 pg/mL
- Continued current cardiac medications

June 2021:

- Repeat echo: EF back to 55%
- Exercise tolerance improving
- Weight: 178 lbs
- BP: 120/78, HR: 64

- eGFR: 68 mL/min (mild CKD Stage 2)

patientHistory:
PATIENT MEDICAL RECORD
Patient ID: MRN-45892
Name: John Anderson
DOB: 03/15/1978 (Age: 45)
Gender: Male
Blood Type: O+

MEDICAL HISTORY SUMMARY:

2015 - INITIAL PRESENTATION
- Chief Complaint: Routine physical examination
- Vital Signs: BP 138/88, HR 78, Temp 98.6°F
- Diagnosed with Stage 1 Hypertension
- Prescribed: Lisinopril 10mg daily
- Lifestyle modifications recommended: diet, exercise
- Family History: Father had MI at age 62, Mother has Type 2 Diabetes

2016 - FOLLOW-UP VISITS
January 2016:
- BP improved to 128/82 on medication
- Weight: 195 lbs, BMI 28.5 (overweight)
- Lipid Panel: Total Cholesterol 245, LDL 165, HDL 38, Triglycerides 210
- Started Atorvastatin 20mg for hyperlipidemia
- Advised smoking cessation (1 pack/day for 20 years)

June 2016:
- Complained of occasional chest discomfort with exertion
- EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, no acute changes
- Stress test ordered - Result: Negative for ischemia
- Continued current medications

2017 - DIABETES DIAGNOSIS
March 2017:
- Fasting glucose: 156 mg/dL, HbA1c: 7.2%
- Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Started Metformin 500mg twice daily
- Referred to diabetes education program
- Dietary consultation completed

September 2017:
- HbA1c improved to 6.8%
- Weight: 188 lbs (lost 7 lbs)
- BP: 125/80, well controlled
- Lipids improving: LDL 142, HDL 42

2018 - CARDIAC WORKUP
February 2018:
- Presented with increased chest discomfort
- Described as pressure-like, substernal, radiating to left arm
- Occurs with moderate exertion, relieved by rest
- Duration: 5-10 minutes per episode
- Frequency: 2-3 times per week

Diagnostic Tests Performed:
- EKG: T-wave flattening in leads V4-V6
- Echocardiogram: Mild LV hypertrophy, EF 55%, no wall motion abnormalities
- Nuclear stress test: Small area of reversible ischemia in LAD territory
- Coronary angiography: 60% stenosis in mid-LAD, 40% stenosis in RCA

Treatment Plan:
- Medical management chosen over intervention
- Added Metoprolol 50mg twice daily
- Added Aspirin 81mg daily
- Added Nitroglycerin SL PRN for chest pain
- Increased Atorvastatin to 40mg
- Cardiac rehabilitation program enrollment

2019 - STABLE ANGINA MANAGEMENT
Quarterly follow-ups throughout 2019:
- Angina episodes reduced to 1-2 per month
- Medications well tolerated
- Weight: 182 lbs (continued weight loss)
- BP: 118/76, HR: 62 (on beta blocker)
- HbA1c: 6.5%, glucose control improved
- Lipids: Total 180, LDL 98, HDL 48, Triglycerides 170
- Successfully quit smoking (6 months smoke-free)

2020 - COVID-19 PANDEMIC
March 2020:
- Telehealth visits initiated due to pandemic
- Medications refilled, no changes
- Patient reports increased stress, working from home
- Exercise routine disrupted

August 2020:
- Tested positive for COVID-19
- Mild symptoms: fever, cough, fatigue
- Managed at home with supportive care
- Full recovery after 2 weeks
- No cardiac complications noted

2021 - POST-COVID MONITORING
January 2021:
- Post-COVID follow-up
- Complained of increased fatigue
- Repeat echocardiogram: EF 52%, mild decrease from baseline
- Troponin: Normal
- BNP: Mildly elevated at 125 pg/mL
- Continued current cardiac medications

June 2021:
- Fatigue improved
- Repeat echo: EF back to 55%
- Exercise tolerance improving
- Weight: 178 lbs
- BP: 120/78, HR: 64

2022 - MEDICATION ADJUSTMENTS
February 2022:
- Routine labs: Creatinine slightly elevated at 1.3 (baseline 1.0)
- eGFR: 68 mL/min (mild CKD Stage 2)
- Lisinopril dose reduced to 5mg due to kidney function
- Metformin continued (safe at this eGFR level)

July 2022:
- Complained of occasional palpitations
- 24-hour Holter monitor: Rare PVCs, no sustained arrhythmias
- Electrolytes: Normal
- Thyroid function: Normal
- Reassured, no treatment needed

November 2022:
- Annual cardiac stress test: Negative for new ischemia
- Coronary CTA: Stable CAD, no progression
- LAD stenosis unchanged at 60%
- No new lesions identified

2023 - RECENT HISTORY
January 2023:
- Routine follow-up
- All medications continued
- Weight: 175 lbs (BMI 25.5 - normal range achieved)
- BP: 122/80, HR: 66
- HbA1c: 6.3% (excellent control)
- Lipids: LDL 85, HDL 52 (at goal)

April 2023:
- Presented with chest pain episode lasting 20 minutes
- Pain resolved spontaneously before arrival
- EKG: No acute changes
- Troponin: Negative x2 (0 and 3 hours)
- Observation overnight, stress test next day: Negative
- Diagnosed as unstable angina vs GERD
- Added Omeprazole 20mg daily
- Increased Metoprolol to 75mg twice daily

August 2023:
- Doing well on current regimen
- Chest pain episodes rare (1 per month)
- Usually related to heavy meals or stress
- Responds well to nitroglycerin when needed
- Exercise tolerance good - walks 2 miles daily

CURRENT MEDICATIONS (as of December 2023):
1. Lisinopril 5mg daily (hypertension, cardioprotection)

3. Atorvastatin 40mg nightly (hyperlipidemia)

7. Nitroglycerin 0.4mg SL PRN (angina)

Described as crushing, substernal pressure 9/10 severity, radiating to left arm and jaw. Associated with profuse diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. Pain not relieved by 3 doses of home nitroglycerin. Patient appears anxious and in distress. Skin cool, clammy. EMS administered aspirin 324mg and oxygen 4L NC en route.

VITAL SIGNS ON ARRIVAL:
BP: 168/95 mmHg
HR: 102 bpm (regular)
RR: 22 breaths/min
SpO2: 94% on 4L O2
Temp: 98.2°F

PHYSICAL EXAM:

- Cardiovascular: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs
- Respiratory: Mild tachypnea, clear lung sounds bilaterally
- Extremities: Cool, clammy, pulses intact

IMMEDIATE CONCERNS:

- Known CAD with 60% LAD stenosis
- Multiple cardiac risk factors
- Prolonged chest pain unresponsive to nitroglycerin
- Hemodynamically stable but symptomatic

patientHistory:
PATIENT MEDICAL RECORD
Patient ID: MRN-45892
Name: John Anderson
DOB: 03/15/1978 (Age: 45)
Gender: Male
Blood Type: O+

MEDICAL HISTORY SUMMARY:

2015 - INITIAL PRESENTATION
- Chief Complaint: Routine physical examination
- Vital Signs: BP 138/88, HR 78, Temp 98.6°F
- Diagnosed with Stage 1 Hypertension
- Prescribed: Lisinopril 10mg daily
- Lifestyle modifications recommended: diet, exercise
- Family History: Father had MI at age 62, Mother has Type 2 Diabetes

2016 - FOLLOW-UP VISITS
January 2016:
- BP improved to 128/82 on medication
- Weight: 195 lbs, BMI 28.5 (overweight)
- Lipid Panel: Total Cholesterol 245, LDL 165, HDL 38, Triglycerides 210
- Started Atorvastatin 20mg for hyperlipidemia
- Advised smoking cessation (1 pack/day for 20 years)

June 2016:
- Complained of occasional chest discomfort with exertion
- EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, no acute changes
- Stress test ordered - Result: Negative for ischemia
- Continued current medications

2017 - DIABETES DIAGNOSIS
March 2017:
- Fasting glucose: 156 mg/dL, HbA1c: 7.2%
- Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Started Metformin 500mg twice daily
- Referred to diabetes education program
- Dietary consultation completed

September 2017:
- HbA1c improved to 6.8%
- Weight: 188 lbs (lost 7 lbs)
- BP: 125/80, well controlled
- Lipids improving: LDL 142, HDL 42

2018 - CARDIAC WORKUP
February 2018:
- Presented with increased chest discomfort
- Described as pressure-like, substernal, radiating to left arm
- Occurs with moderate exertion, relieved by rest
- Duration: 5-10 minutes per episode
- Frequency: 2-3 times per week

Diagnostic Tests Performed:
- EKG: T-wave flattening in leads V4-V6
- Echocardiogram: Mild LV hypertrophy, EF 55%, no wall motion abnormalities
- Nuclear stress test: Small area of reversible ischemia in LAD territory
- Coronary angiography: 60% stenosis in mid-LAD, 40% stenosis in RCA

Treatment Plan:
- Medical management chosen over intervention
- Added Metoprolol 50mg twice daily
- Added Aspirin 81mg daily
- Added Nitroglycerin SL PRN for chest pain
- Increased Atorvastatin to 40mg
- Cardiac rehabilitation program enrollment

2019 - STABLE ANGINA MANAGEMENT
Quarterly follow-ups throughout 2019:
- Angina episodes reduced to 1-2 per month
- Medications well tolerated
- Weight: 182 lbs (continued weight loss)
- BP: 118/76, HR: 62 (on beta blocker)
- HbA1c: 6.5%, glucose control improved
- Lipids: Total 180, LDL 98, HDL 48, Triglycerides 170
- Successfully quit smoking (6 months smoke-free)

2020 - COVID-19 PANDEMIC
March 2020:
- Telehealth visits initiated due to pandemic
- Medications refilled, no changes
- Patient reports increased stress, working from home
- Exercise routine disrupted

August 2020:
- Tested positive for COVID-19
- Mild symptoms: fever, cough, fatigue
- Managed at home with supportive care
- Full recovery after 2 weeks
- No cardiac complications noted

2021 - POST-COVID MONITORING
January 2021:
- Post-COVID follow-up
- Complained of increased fatigue
- Repeat echocardiogram: EF 52%, mild decrease from baseline
- Troponin: Normal
- BNP: Mildly elevated at 125 pg/mL
- Continued current cardiac medications

June 2021:
- Fatigue improved
- Repeat echo: EF back to 55%
- Exercise tolerance improving
- Weight: 178 lbs
- BP: 120/78, HR: 64

2022 - MEDICATION ADJUSTMENTS
February 2022:
- Routine labs: Creatinine slightly elevated at 1.3 (baseline 1.0)
- eGFR: 68 mL/min (mild CKD Stage 2)
- Lisinopril dose reduced to 5mg due to kidney function
- Metformin continued (safe at this eGFR level)

July 2022:
- Complained of occasional palpitations
- 24-hour Holter monitor: Rare PVCs, no sustained arrhythmias
- Electrolytes: Normal
- Thyroid function: Normal
- Reassured, no treatment needed

November 2022:
- Annual cardiac stress test: Negative for new ischemia
- Coronary CTA: Stable CAD, no progression
- LAD stenosis unchanged at 60%
- No new lesions identified

2023 - RECENT HISTORY
January 2023:
- Routine follow-up
- All medications continued
- Weight: 175 lbs (BMI 25.5 - normal range achieved)
- BP: 122/80, HR: 66
- HbA1c: 6.3% (excellent control)
- Lipids: LDL 85, HDL 52 (at goal)

April 2023:
- Presented with chest pain episode lasting 20 minutes
- Pain resolved spontaneously before arrival
- EKG: No acute changes
- Troponin: Negative x2 (0 and 3 hours)
- Observation overnight, stress test next day: Negative
- Diagnosed as unstable angina vs GERD
- Added Omeprazole 20mg daily
- Increased Metoprolol to 75mg twice daily

August 2023:
- Doing well on current regimen
- Chest pain episodes rare (1 per month)
- Usually related to heavy meals or stress
- Responds well to nitroglycerin when needed
- Exercise tolerance good - walks 2 miles daily

CURRENT MEDICATIONS (as of December 2023):
1. Lisinopril 5mg daily (hypertension, cardioprotection)
2. Metoprolol 75mg twice daily (angina, hypertension)
3. Atorvastatin 40mg nightly (hyperlipidemia)
4. Aspirin 81mg daily (antiplatelet)
5. Metformin 500mg twice daily (diabetes)
6. Omeprazole 20mg daily (GERD)
7. Nitroglycerin 0.4mg SL PRN (angina)

ALLERGIES:
- Penicillin (rash)
- Sulfa drugs (hives)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Occupation: Software engineer (sedentary)
- Marital Status: Married, 2 children
- Alcohol: Occasional (1-2 drinks per week)
- Tobacco: Former smoker, quit 2019
- Exercise: Walks daily, light weights 3x/week
- Diet: Following cardiac diet, reduced sodium

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father: MI age 62, CABG age 65, died age 72 (heart failure)
- Mother: Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, alive age 73
- Brother: Hyperlipidemia, age 48
- Sister: Healthy, age 42

- CBC: WBC 7.2, Hgb 14.5, Plt 245 (normal)

- Coronary CTA (2022): Known CAD, stable

patientHistory:
PATIENT MEDICAL RECORD
Patient ID: MRN-45892
Name: John Anderson
DOB: 03/15/1978 (Age: 45)
Gender: Male
Blood Type: O+

MEDICAL HISTORY SUMMARY:

2015 - INITIAL PRESENTATION
- Chief Complaint: Routine physical examination
- Vital Signs: BP 138/88, HR 78, Temp 98.6°F
- Diagnosed with Stage 1 Hypertension
- Prescribed: Lisinopril 10mg daily
- Lifestyle modifications recommended: diet, exercise
- Family History: Father had MI at age 62, Mother has Type 2 Diabetes

2016 - FOLLOW-UP VISITS
January 2016:
- BP improved to 128/82 on medication
- Weight: 195 lbs, BMI 28.5 (overweight)
- Lipid Panel: Total Cholesterol 245, LDL 165, HDL 38, Triglycerides 210
- Started Atorvastatin 20mg for hyperlipidemia
- Advised smoking cessation (1 pack/day for 20 years)

June 2016:
- Complained of occasional chest discomfort with exertion
- EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, no acute changes
- Stress test ordered - Result: Negative for ischemia
- Continued current medications

2017 - DIABETES DIAGNOSIS
March 2017:
- Fasting glucose: 156 mg/dL, HbA1c: 7.2%
- Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Started Metformin 500mg twice daily
- Referred to diabetes education program
- Dietary consultation completed

September 2017:
- HbA1c improved to 6.8%
- Weight: 188 lbs (lost 7 lbs)
- BP: 125/80, well controlled
- Lipids improving: LDL 142, HDL 42

2018 - CARDIAC WORKUP
February 2018:
- Presented with increased chest discomfort
- Described as pressure-like, substernal, radiating to left arm
- Occurs with moderate exertion, relieved by rest
- Duration: 5-10 minutes per episode
- Frequency: 2-3 times per week

Diagnostic Tests Performed:
- EKG: T-wave flattening in leads V4-V6
- Echocardiogram: Mild LV hypertrophy, EF 55%, no wall motion abnormalities
- Nuclear stress test: Small area of reversible ischemia in LAD territory
- Coronary angiography: 60% stenosis in mid-LAD, 40% stenosis in RCA

Treatment Plan:
- Medical management chosen over intervention
- Added Metoprolol 50mg twice daily
- Added Aspirin 81mg daily
- Added Nitroglycerin SL PRN for chest pain
- Increased Atorvastatin to 40mg
- Cardiac rehabilitation program enrollment

2019 - STABLE ANGINA MANAGEMENT
Quarterly follow-ups throughout 2019:
- Angina episodes reduced to 1-2 per month
- Medications well tolerated
- Weight: 182 lbs (continued weight loss)
- BP: 118/76, HR: 62 (on beta blocker)
- HbA1c: 6.5%, glucose control improved
- Lipids: Total 180, LDL 98, HDL 48, Triglycerides 170
- Successfully quit smoking (6 months smoke-free)

2020 - COVID-19 PANDEMIC
March 2020:
- Telehealth visits initiated due to pandemic
- Medications refilled, no changes
- Patient reports increased stress, working from home
- Exercise routine disrupted

August 2020:
- Tested positive for COVID-19
- Mild symptoms: fever, cough, fatigue
- Managed at home with supportive care
- Full recovery after 2 weeks
- No cardiac complications noted

2021 - POST-COVID MONITORING
January 2021:
- Post-COVID follow-up
- Complained of increased fatigue
- Repeat echocardiogram: EF 52%, mild decrease from baseline
- Troponin: Normal
- BNP: Mildly elevated at 125 pg/mL
- Continued current cardiac medications

June 2021:
- Fatigue improved
- Repeat echo: EF back to 55%
- Exercise tolerance improving
- Weight: 178 lbs
- BP: 120/78, HR: 64

2022 - MEDICATION ADJUSTMENTS
February 2022:
- Routine labs: Creatinine slightly elevated at 1.3 (baseline 1.0)
- eGFR: 68 mL/min (mild CKD Stage 2)
- Lisinopril dose reduced to 5mg due to kidney function
- Metformin continued (safe at this eGFR level)

July 2022:
- Complained of occasional palpitations
- 24-hour Holter monitor: Rare PVCs, no sustained arrhythmias
- Electrolytes: Normal
- Thyroid function: Normal
- Reassured, no treatment needed

November 2022:
- Annual cardiac stress test: Negative for new ischemia
- Coronary CTA: Stable CAD, no progression
- LAD stenosis unchanged at 60%
- No new lesions identified

2023 - RECENT HISTORY
January 2023:
- Routine follow-up
- All medications continued
- Weight: 175 lbs (BMI 25.5 - normal range achieved)
- BP: 122/80, HR: 66
- HbA1c: 6.3% (excellent control)
- Lipids: LDL 85, HDL 52 (at goal)

April 2023:
- Presented with chest pain episode lasting 20 minutes
- Pain resolved spontaneously before arrival
- EKG: No acute changes
- Troponin: Negative x2 (0 and 3 hours)
- Observation overnight, stress test next day: Negative
- Diagnosed as unstable angina vs GERD
- Added Omeprazole 20mg daily
- Increased Metoprolol to 75mg twice daily

August 2023:
- Doing well on current regimen
- Chest pain episodes rare (1 per month)
- Usually related to heavy meals or stress
- Responds well to nitroglycerin when needed
- Exercise tolerance good - walks 2 miles daily

CURRENT MEDICATIONS (as of December 2023):
1. Lisinopril 5mg daily (hypertension, cardioprotection)
2. Metoprolol 75mg twice daily (angina, hypertension)
3. Atorvastatin 40mg nightly (hyperlipidemia)
4. Aspirin 81mg daily (antiplatelet)
5. Metformin 500mg twice daily (diabetes)
6. Omeprazole 20mg daily (GERD)
7. Nitroglycerin 0.4mg SL PRN (angina)

ALLERGIES:
- Penicillin (rash)
- Sulfa drugs (hives)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Occupation: Software engineer (sedentary)
- Marital Status: Married, 2 children
- Alcohol: Occasional (1-2 drinks per week)
- Tobacco: Former smoker, quit 2019
- Exercise: Walks daily, light weights 3x/week
- Diet: Following cardiac diet, reduced sodium

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father: MI age 62, CABG age 65, died age 72 (heart failure)
- Mother: Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, alive age 73
- Brother: Hyperlipidemia, age 48
- Sister: Healthy, age 42

RECENT LAB VALUES (November 2023):
- CBC: WBC 7.2, Hgb 14.5, Plt 245 (normal)
- BMP: Na 140, K 4.2, Cl 102, CO2 26, BUN 18, Cr 1.2, Glucose 108
- Lipids: Total 175, LDL 85, HDL 52, Triglycerides 145
- HbA1c: 6.3%
- TSH: 2.1 (normal)
- Liver enzymes: Normal

IMAGING STUDIES ON FILE:
- Chest X-ray (2023): Clear lungs, normal cardiac silhouette
- Echocardiogram (2023): EF 55%, mild LVH, no valvular disease
- Coronary CTA (2022): Known CAD, stable
- Carotid ultrasound (2021): No significant stenosis

PROCEDURES:
- Coronary angiography (2018): 60% LAD stenosis, medical management
- Multiple stress tests (2016-2023): Most recent negative

HOSPITALIZATIONS:
- None for cardiac reasons
- COVID-19 (2020): Managed at home

EMERGENCY DEPARTMENT VISITS:
- April 2023: Chest pain, ruled out MI, discharged
- March 2018: Chest pain evaluation, admitted for cardiac cath

RISK FACTORS:
- Known coronary artery disease
- Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Hypertension (controlled)
- Hyperlipidemia (controlled)
- Former smoker
- Family history of premature CAD
- Mild chronic kidney disease
- Overweight (improved)

FUNCTIONAL STATUS:
- Independent in all ADLs
- Works full-time
- Exercises regularly
- No limitations in daily activities
- Occasional angina with heavy exertion
emergencyDescription:
Patient presenting to ED via EMS with acute onset severe chest pain. Pain started 45 minutes ago while shoveling snow. Described as crushing, substernal pressure 9/10 severity, radiating to left arm and jaw. Associated with profuse diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. Pain not relieved by 3 doses of home nitroglycerin. Patient appears anxious and in distress. Skin cool, clammy. EMS administered aspirin 324mg and oxygen 4L NC en route.

VITAL SIGNS ON ARRIVAL:
BP: 168/95 mmHg
HR: 102 bpm (regular)
RR: 22 breaths/min
SpO2: 94% on 4L O2
Temp: 98.2°F

PHYSICAL EXAM:
- General: Anxious, diaphoretic, in moderate distress
- Cardiovascular: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs
- Respiratory: Mild tachypnea, clear lung sounds bilaterally
- Extremities: Cool, clammy, pulses intact

IMMEDIATE CONCERNS:
- Acute coronary syndrome (STEMI vs NSTEMI)
- Known CAD with 60% LAD stenosis
- Multiple cardiac risk factors
- Prolonged chest pain unresponsive to nitroglycerin
- Hemodynamically stable but symptomatic

emergencyDescription:
Patient presenting to ED via EMS with acute onset severe chest pain. Pain started 45 minutes ago while shoveling snow. Described as crushing, substernal pressure 9/10 severity, radiating to left arm and jaw. Associated with profuse diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. Pain not relieved by 3 doses of home nitroglycerin. Patient appears anxious and in distress. Skin cool, clammy. EMS administered aspirin 324mg and oxygen 4L NC en route.

VITAL SIGNS ON ARRIVAL:
BP: 168/95 mmHg
HR: 102 bpm (regular)
RR: 22 breaths/min
SpO2: 94% on 4L O2
Temp: 98.2°F

PHYSICAL EXAM:
- General: Anxious, diaphoretic, in moderate distress
- Cardiovascular: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs
- Respiratory: Mild tachypnea, clear lung sounds bilaterally
- Extremities: Cool, clammy, pulses intact

IMMEDIATE CONCERNS:
- Acute coronary syndrome (STEMI vs NSTEMI)
- Known CAD with 60% LAD stenosis
- Multiple cardiac risk factors
- Prolonged chest pain unresponsive to nitroglycerin
- Hemodynamically stable but symptomatic

emergencyDescription:
Patient presenting to ED via EMS with acute onset severe chest pain. Pain started 45 minutes ago while shoveling snow. Described as crushing, substernal pressure 9/10 severity, radiating to left arm and jaw. Associated with profuse diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. Pain not relieved by 3 doses of home nitroglycerin. Patient appears anxious and in distress. Skin cool, clammy. EMS administered aspirin 324mg and oxygen 4L NC en route.

VITAL SIGNS ON ARRIVAL:
BP: 168/95 mmHg
HR: 102 bpm (regular)
RR: 22 breaths/min
SpO2: 94% on 4L O2
Temp: 98.2°F

PHYSICAL EXAM:
- General: Anxious, diaphoretic, in moderate distress
- Cardiovascular: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs
- Respiratory: Mild tachypnea, clear lung sounds bilaterally
- Extremities: Cool, clammy, pulses intact

IMMEDIATE CONCERNS:
- Acute coronary syndrome (STEMI vs NSTEMI)
- Known CAD with 60% LAD stenosis
- Multiple cardiac risk factors
- Prolonged chest pain unresponsive to nitroglycerin
- Hemodynamically stable but symptomatic

2015 - INITIAL PRESENTATION

- Lifestyle modifications recommended: diet, exercise
- Family History: Father had MI at age 62, Mother has Type 2 Diabetes

2016 - FOLLOW-UP VISITS
January 2016:
- BP improved to 128/82 on medication
- Weight: 195 lbs, BMI 28.5 (overweight)
- Lipid Panel: Total Cholesterol 245, LDL 165, HDL 38, Triglycerides 210
- Started Atorvastatin 20mg for hyperlipidemia
- Advised smoking cessation (1 pack/day for 20 years)

June 2016:
- Complained of occasional chest discomfort with exertion
- EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, no acute changes
- Stress test ordered - Result: Negative for ischemia
- Continued current medications

2017 - DIABETES DIAGNOSIS
March 2017:
- Fasting glucose: 156 mg/dL, HbA1c: 7.2%
- Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Started Metformin 500mg twice daily
- Referred to diabetes education program
- Dietary consultation completed

September 2017:
- HbA1c improved to 6.8%
- Weight: 188 lbs (lost 7 lbs)
- BP: 125/80, well controlled
- Lipids improving: LDL 142, HDL 42

2018 - CARDIAC WORKUP
February 2018:
- Presented with increased chest discomfort
- Described as pressure-like, substernal, radiating to left arm
- Occurs with moderate exertion, relieved by rest
- Duration: 5-10 minutes per episode
- Frequency: 2-3 times per week

Diagnostic Tests Performed:
- EKG: T-wave flattening in leads V4-V6
- Echocardiogram: Mild LV hypertrophy, EF 55%, no wall motion abnormalities
- Nuclear stress test: Small area of reversible ischemia in LAD territory
- Coronary angiography: 60% stenosis in mid-LAD, 40% stenosis in RCA

Treatment Plan:
- Medical management chosen over intervention
- Added Metoprolol 50mg twice daily
- Added Aspirin 81mg daily
- Added Nitroglycerin SL PRN for chest pain
- Increased Atorvastatin to 40mg
- Cardiac rehabilitation program enrollment

2019 - STABLE ANGINA MANAGEMENT

- Angina episodes reduced to 1-2 per month
- Medications well tolerated
- Weight: 182 lbs (continued weight loss)
- BP: 118/76, HR: 62 (on beta blocker)
- HbA1c: 6.5%, glucose control improved
- Lipids: Total 180, LDL 98, HDL 48, Triglycerides 170
- Successfully quit smoking (6 months smoke-free)

2020 - COVID-19 PANDEMIC
March 2020:
- Telehealth visits initiated due to pandemic
- Medications refilled, no changes
- Patient reports increased stress, working from home
- Exercise routine disrupted

August 2020:

- Mild symptoms: fever, cough, fatigue
- Managed at home with supportive care
- Full recovery after 2 weeks
- No cardiac complications noted

emergencyDescription:
Patient presenting to ED via EMS with acute onset severe chest pain. Pain started 45 minutes ago while shoveling snow. Described as crushing, substernal pressure 9/10 severity, radiating to left arm and jaw. Associated with profuse diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. Pain not relieved by 3 doses of home nitroglycerin. Patient appears anxious and in distress. Skin cool, clammy. EMS administered aspirin 324mg and oxygen 4L NC en route.

VITAL SIGNS ON ARRIVAL:
BP: 168/95 mmHg
HR: 102 bpm (regular)
RR: 22 breaths/min
SpO2: 94% on 4L O2
Temp: 98.2°F

PHYSICAL EXAM:
- General: Anxious, diaphoretic, in moderate distress
- Cardiovascular: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs
- Respiratory: Mild tachypnea, clear lung sounds bilaterally
- Extremities: Cool, clammy, pulses intact

IMMEDIATE CONCERNS:
- Acute coronary syndrome (STEMI vs NSTEMI)
- Known CAD with 60% LAD stenosis
- Multiple cardiac risk factors
- Prolonged chest pain unresponsive to nitroglycerin
- Hemodynamically stable but symptomatic

triageResult.immediate_action: Perform immediate ECG and cardiac enzymes

patientHistory:
PATIENT MEDICAL RECORD
Patient ID: MRN-45892
Name: John Anderson
DOB: 03/15/1978 (Age: 45)
Gender: Male
Blood Type: O+

MEDICAL HISTORY SUMMARY:

2015 - INITIAL PRESENTATION
- Chief Complaint: Routine physical examination
- Vital Signs: BP 138/88, HR 78, Temp 98.6°F
- Diagnosed with Stage 1 Hypertension
- Prescribed: Lisinopril 10mg daily
- Lifestyle modifications recommended: diet, exercise
- Family History: Father had MI at age 62, Mother has Type 2 Diabetes

2016 - FOLLOW-UP VISITS
January 2016:
- BP improved to 128/82 on medication
- Weight: 195 lbs, BMI 28.5 (overweight)
- Lipid Panel: Total Cholesterol 245, LDL 165, HDL 38, Triglycerides 210
- Started Atorvastatin 20mg for hyperlipidemia
- Advised smoking cessation (1 pack/day for 20 years)

June 2016:
- Complained of occasional chest discomfort with exertion
- EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, no acute changes
- Stress test ordered - Result: Negative for ischemia
- Continued current medications

2017 - DIABETES DIAGNOSIS
March 2017:
- Fasting glucose: 156 mg/dL, HbA1c: 7.2%
- Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Started Metformin 500mg twice daily
- Referred to diabetes education program
- Dietary consultation completed

September 2017:
- HbA1c improved to 6.8%
- Weight: 188 lbs (lost 7 lbs)
- BP: 125/80, well controlled
- Lipids improving: LDL 142, HDL 42

2018 - CARDIAC WORKUP
February 2018:
- Presented with increased chest discomfort
- Described as pressure-like, substernal, radiating to left arm
- Occurs with moderate exertion, relieved by rest
- Duration: 5-10 minutes per episode
- Frequency: 2-3 times per week

Diagnostic Tests Performed:
- EKG: T-wave flattening in leads V4-V6
- Echocardiogram: Mild LV hypertrophy, EF 55%, no wall motion abnormalities
- Nuclear stress test: Small area of reversible ischemia in LAD territory
- Coronary angiography: 60% stenosis in mid-LAD, 40% stenosis in RCA

Treatment Plan:
- Medical management chosen over intervention
- Added Metoprolol 50mg twice daily
- Added Aspirin 81mg daily
- Added Nitroglycerin SL PRN for chest pain
- Increased Atorvastatin to 40mg
- Cardiac rehabilitation program enrollment

2019 - STABLE ANGINA MANAGEMENT
Quarterly follow-ups throughout 2019:
- Angina episodes reduced to 1-2 per month
- Medications well tolerated
- Weight: 182 lbs (continued weight loss)
- BP: 118/76, HR: 62 (on beta blocker)
- HbA1c: 6.5%, glucose control improved
- Lipids: Total 180, LDL 98, HDL 48, Triglycerides 170
- Successfully quit smoking (6 months smoke-free)

2020 - COVID-19 PANDEMIC
March 2020:
- Telehealth visits initiated due to pandemic
- Medications refilled, no changes
- Patient reports increased stress, working from home
- Exercise routine disrupted

August 2020:
- Tested positive for COVID-19
- Mild symptoms: fever, cough, fatigue
- Managed at home with supportive care
- Full recovery after 2 weeks
- No cardiac complications noted

2021 - POST-COVID MONITORING
January 2021:
- Post-COVID follow-up
- Complained of increased fatigue
- Repeat echocardiogram: EF 52%, mild decrease from baseline
- Troponin: Normal
- BNP: Mildly elevated at 125 pg/mL
- Continued current cardiac medications

June 2021:
- Fatigue improved
- Repeat echo: EF back to 55%
- Exercise tolerance improving
- Weight: 178 lbs
- BP: 120/78, HR: 64

2022 - MEDICATION ADJUSTMENTS
February 2022:
- Routine labs: Creatinine slightly elevated at 1.3 (baseline 1.0)
- eGFR: 68 mL/min (mild CKD Stage 2)
- Lisinopril dose reduced to 5mg due to kidney function
- Metformin continued (safe at this eGFR level)

emergencyDescription:
EMERGENCY DEPARTMENT PRESENTATION

Patient arrived via ambulance with sudden onset severe chest pain. Pain began approximately 35 minutes prior while climbing stairs. Described as heavy, pressure-like discomfort in the center of the chest, rated 8/10 in intensity, radiating to the right arm and neck. Associated symptoms include sweating, mild nausea, and breathlessness. No relief after taking 2 doses of sublingual nitroglycerin at home. Patient appears restless and uncomfortable. Skin noted to be pale and moist. Paramedics administered aspirin 300 mg and oxygen 3L via nasal cannula during transport.

--------------------------------------------------

VITAL SIGNS ON ARRIVAL:
BP: 162/92 mmHg
HR: 98 bpm (regular)
RR: 20 breaths/min
SpO2: 95% on 3L O2
Temp: 98.4°F

--------------------------------------------------

PHYSICAL EXAM:
- General: Restless, diaphoretic, mild distress
- Cardiovascular: Mild tachycardia, regular rhythm, no added sounds
- Respiratory: Slightly increased work of breathing, lungs clear
- Extremities: Cool peripheries, capillary refill slightly delayed, pulses present

--------------------------------------------------

IMMEDIATE CLINICAL CONCERNS:
- Suspected Acute Coronary Syndrome (possible NSTEMI/unstable angina)
- History of coronary artery disease (moderate LAD involvement)
- Multiple cardiovascular risk factors
- Persistent chest pain not responding to nitroglycerin
- Currently stable vitals but high-risk presentation

patientHistory:
PATIENT MEDICAL RECORD
Patient ID: MRN-45892
Name: John Anderson
DOB: 03/15/1978 (Age: 45)
Gender: Male
Blood Type: O+

MEDICAL HISTORY SUMMARY:

2015 - INITIAL PRESENTATION
- Chief Complaint: Routine physical examination
- Vital Signs: BP 138/88, HR 78, Temp 98.6°F
- Diagnosed with Stage 1 Hypertension
- Prescribed: Lisinopril 10mg daily
- Lifestyle modifications recommended: diet, exercise
- Family History: Father had MI at age 62, Mother has Type 2 Diabetes

2016 - FOLLOW-UP VISITS
January 2016:
- BP improved to 128/82 on medication
- Weight: 195 lbs, BMI 28.5 (overweight)
- Lipid Panel: Total Cholesterol 245, LDL 165, HDL 38, Triglycerides 210
- Started Atorvastatin 20mg for hyperlipidemia
- Advised smoking cessation (1 pack/day for 20 years)

June 2016:
- Complained of occasional chest discomfort with exertion
- EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, no acute changes
- Stress test ordered - Result: Negative for ischemia
- Continued current medications

2017 - DIABETES DIAGNOSIS
March 2017:
- Fasting glucose: 156 mg/dL, HbA1c: 7.2%
- Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Started Metformin 500mg twice daily
- Referred to diabetes education program
- Dietary consultation completed

September 2017:
- HbA1c improved to 6.8%
- Weight: 188 lbs (lost 7 lbs)
- BP: 125/80, well controlled
- Lipids improving: LDL 142, HDL 42

2018 - CARDIAC WORKUP
February 2018:
- Presented with increased chest discomfort
- Described as pressure-like, substernal, radiating to left arm
- Occurs with moderate exertion, relieved by rest
- Duration: 5-10 minutes per episode
- Frequency: 2-3 times per week

Diagnostic Tests Performed:
- EKG: T-wave flattening in leads V4-V6
- Echocardiogram: Mild LV hypertrophy, EF 55%, no wall motion abnormalities
- Nuclear stress test: Small area of reversible ischemia in LAD territory
- Coronary angiography: 60% stenosis in mid-LAD, 40% stenosis in RCA

Treatment Plan:
- Medical management chosen over intervention
- Added Metoprolol 50mg twice daily
- Added Aspirin 81mg daily
- Added Nitroglycerin SL PRN for chest pain
- Increased Atorvastatin to 40mg
- Cardiac rehabilitation program enrollment

2019 - STABLE ANGINA MANAGEMENT
Quarterly follow-ups throughout 2019:
- Angina episodes reduced to 1-2 per month
- Medications well tolerated
- Weight: 182 lbs (continued weight loss)
- BP: 118/76, HR: 62 (on beta blocker)
- HbA1c: 6.5%, glucose control improved
- Lipids: Total 180, LDL 98, HDL 48, Triglycerides 170
- Successfully quit smoking (6 months smoke-free)

2020 - COVID-19 PANDEMIC
March 2020:
- Telehealth visits initiated due to pandemic
- Medications refilled, no changes
- Patient reports increased stress, working from home
- Exercise routine disrupted

August 2020:
- Tested positive for COVID-19
- Mild symptoms: fever, cough, fatigue
- Managed at home with supportive care
- Full recovery after 2 weeks
- No cardiac complications noted

2021 - POST-COVID MONITORING
January 2021:
- Post-COVID follow-up
- Complained of increased fatigue
- Repeat echocardiogram: EF 52%, mild decrease from baseline
- Troponin: Normal
- BNP: Mildly elevated at 125 pg/mL
- Continued current cardiac medications

June 2021:
- Fatigue improved
- Repeat echo: EF back to 55%
- Exercise tolerance improving
- Weight: 178 lbs
- BP: 120/78, HR: 64

2022 - MEDICATION ADJUSTMENTS
February 2022:
- Routine labs: Creatinine slightly elevated at 1.3 (baseline 1.0)
- eGFR: 68 mL/min (mild CKD Stage 2)
- Lisinopril dose reduced to 5mg due to kidney function
- Metformin continued (safe at this eGFR level)

July 2022:
- Complained of occasional palpitations
- 24-hour Holter monitor: Rare PVCs, no sustained arrhythmias
- Electrolytes: Normal
- Thyroid function: Normal
- Reassured, no treatment needed

November 2022:
- Annual cardiac stress test: Negative for new ischemia
- Coronary CTA: Stable CAD, no progression
- LAD stenosis unchanged at 60%
- No new lesions identified

2023 - RECENT HISTORY
January 2023:
- Routine follow-up
- All medications continued
- Weight: 175 lbs (BMI 25.5 - normal range achieved)
- BP: 122/80, HR: 66
- HbA1c: 6.3% (excellent control)
- Lipids: LDL 85, HDL 52 (at goal)

April 2023:
- Presented with chest pain episode lasting 20 minutes
- Pain resolved spontaneously before arrival
- EKG: No acute changes
- Troponin: Negative x2 (0 and 3 hours)
- Observation overnight, stress test next day: Negative
- Diagnosed as unstable angina vs GERD
- Added Omeprazole 20mg daily
- Increased Metoprolol to 75mg twice daily

August 2023:
- Doing well on current regimen
- Chest pain episodes rare (1 per month)
- Usually related to heavy meals or stress
- Responds well to nitroglycerin when needed
- Exercise tolerance good - walks 2 miles daily

CURRENT MEDICATIONS (as of December 2023):
1. Lisinopril 5mg daily (hypertension, cardioprotection)
2. Metoprolol 75mg twice daily (angina, hypertension)
3. Atorvastatin 40mg nightly (hyperlipidemia)
4. Aspirin 81mg daily (antiplatelet)
5. Metformin 500mg twice daily (diabetes)
6. Omeprazole 20mg daily (GERD)
7. Nitroglycerin 0.4mg SL PRN (angina)

ALLERGIES:
- Penicillin (rash)
- Sulfa drugs (hives)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Occupation: Software engineer (sedentary)
- Marital Status: Married, 2 children

- Tobacco: Former smoker, quit 2019

- Diet: Following cardiac diet, reduced sodium

emergencyDescription:
Patient presenting to ED via EMS with acute onset severe chest pain. Pain started 45 minutes ago while shoveling snow. Described as crushing, substernal pressure 9/10 severity, radiating to left arm and jaw. Associated with profuse diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. VITAL SIGNS ON ARRIVAL:
BP: 168/95 mmHg
HR: 102 bpm (regular)
RR: 22 breaths/min
SpO2: 94% on 4L O2
Temp: 98.2°F

PHYSICAL EXAM:
- General: Anxious, diaphoretic, in moderate distress
- Cardiovascular: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs
- Respiratory: Mild tachypnea, clear lung sounds bilaterally
- Extremities: Cool, clammy, pulses intact

IMMEDIATE CONCERNS:
- Acute coronary syndrome (STEMI vs NSTEMI)
- Known CAD with 60% LAD stenosis
- Multiple cardiac risk factors
- Prolonged chest pain unresponsive to nitroglycerin
- Hemodynamically stable but symptomatic

- Started Atorvastatin 20mg for hyperlipidemia
- Advised smoking cessation (1 pack/day for 20 years)

June 2016:
- Complained of occasional chest discomfort with exertion
- EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, no acute changes

- Continued current medications

2017 - DIABETES DIAGNOSIS

- Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Started Metformin 500mg twice daily

- Dietary consultation completed

September 2017:

- Weight: 188 lbs (lost 7 lbs)

- Frequency: 2-3 times per week

patientHistory:
PATIENT MEDICAL RECORD
Patient ID: MRN-45892
Name: John Anderson
DOB: 03/15/1978 (Age: 45)
Gender: Male
Blood Type: O+

MEDICAL HISTORY SUMMARY:

2015 - INITIAL PRESENTATION
- Chief Complaint: Routine physical examination
- Vital Signs: BP 138/88, HR 78, Temp 98.6°F
- Diagnosed with Stage 1 Hypertension
- Prescribed: Lisinopril 10mg daily
- Lifestyle modifications recommended: diet, exercise
- Family History: Father had MI at age 62, Mother has Type 2 Diabetes

2016 - FOLLOW-UP VISITS
January 2016:
- BP improved to 128/82 on medication
- Weight: 195 lbs, BMI 28.5 (overweight)
- Lipid Panel: Total Cholesterol 245, LDL 165, HDL 38, Triglycerides 210
- Started Atorvastatin 20mg for hyperlipidemia
- Advised smoking cessation (1 pack/day for 20 years)

June 2016:
- Complained of occasional chest discomfort with exertion
- EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, no acute changes
- Stress test ordered - Result: Negative for ischemia
- Continued current medications

2017 - DIABETES DIAGNOSIS
March 2017:
- Fasting glucose: 156 mg/dL, HbA1c: 7.2%
- Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Started Metformin 500mg twice daily
- Referred to diabetes education program
- Dietary consultation completed

September 2017:
- HbA1c improved to 6.8%
- Weight: 188 lbs (lost 7 lbs)
- BP: 125/80, well controlled
- Lipids improving: LDL 142, HDL 42

2018 - CARDIAC WORKUP
February 2018:
- Presented with increased chest discomfort
- Described as pressure-like, substernal, radiating to left arm
- Occurs with moderate exertion, relieved by rest
- Duration: 5-10 minutes per episode
- Frequency: 2-3 times per week

Diagnostic Tests Performed:
- EKG: T-wave flattening in leads V4-V6
- Echocardiogram: Mild LV hypertrophy, EF 55%, no wall motion abnormalities
- Nuclear stress test: Small area of reversible ischemia in LAD territory
- Coronary angiography: 60% stenosis in mid-LAD, 40% stenosis in RCA

Treatment Plan:
- Medical management chosen over intervention
- Added Metoprolol 50mg twice daily
- Added Aspirin 81mg daily
- Added Nitroglycerin SL PRN for chest pain
- Increased Atorvastatin to 40mg
- Cardiac rehabilitation program enrollment

2019 - STABLE ANGINA MANAGEMENT
Quarterly follow-ups throughout 2019:
- Angina episodes reduced to 1-2 per month
- Medications well tolerated
- Weight: 182 lbs (continued weight loss)
- BP: 118/76, HR: 62 (on beta blocker)
- HbA1c: 6.5%, glucose control improved
- Lipids: Total 180, LDL 98, HDL 48, Triglycerides 170
- Successfully quit smoking (6 months smoke-free)

2020 - COVID-19 PANDEMIC
March 2020:
- Telehealth visits initiated due to pandemic
- Medications refilled, no changes
- Patient reports increased stress, working from home
- Exercise routine disrupted

August 2020:
- Tested positive for COVID-19
- Mild symptoms: fever, cough, fatigue
- Managed at home with supportive care
- Full recovery after 2 weeks
- No cardiac complications noted

2021 - POST-COVID MONITORING
January 2021:
- Post-COVID follow-up
- Complained of increased fatigue
- Repeat echocardiogram: EF 52%, mild decrease from baseline
- Troponin: Normal
- BNP: Mildly elevated at 125 pg/mL
- Continued current cardiac medications

June 2021:
- Fatigue improved
- Repeat echo: EF back to 55%
- Exercise tolerance improving
- Weight: 178 lbs
- BP: 120/78, HR: 64

2022 - MEDICATION ADJUSTMENTS
February 2022:
- Routine labs: Creatinine slightly elevated at 1.3 (baseline 1.0)
- eGFR: 68 mL/min (mild CKD Stage 2)
- Lisinopril dose reduced to 5mg due to kidney function
- Metformin continued (safe at this eGFR level)

July 2022:
- Complained of occasional palpitations
- 24-hour Holter monitor: Rare PVCs, no sustained arrhythmias
- Electrolytes: Normal
- Thyroid function: Normal
- Reassured, no treatment needed

November 2022:
- Annual cardiac stress test: Negative for new ischemia
- Coronary CTA: Stable CAD, no progression
- LAD stenosis unchanged at 60%
- No new lesions identified

2023 - RECENT HISTORY
January 2023:
- Routine follow-up
- All medications continued
- Weight: 175 lbs (BMI 25.5 - normal range achieved)
- BP: 122/80, HR: 66
- HbA1c: 6.3% (excellent control)
- Lipids: LDL 85, HDL 52 (at goal)

April 2023:
- Presented with chest pain episode lasting 20 minutes
- Pain resolved spontaneously before arrival
- EKG: No acute changes
- Troponin: Negative x2 (0 and 3 hours)
- Observation overnight, stress test next day: Negative
- Diagnosed as unstable angina vs GERD
- Added Omeprazole 20mg daily
- Increased Metoprolol to 75mg twice daily

- Doing well on current regimen
- Chest pain episodes rare (1 per month)
- Usually related to heavy meals or stress
- Responds well to nitroglycerin when needed
- Exercise tolerance good - walks 2 miles daily

Described as crushing, substernal pressure 9/10 severity, radiating to left arm and jaw. Associated with profuse diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. Pain not relieved by 3 doses of home nitroglycerin. Patient appears anxious and in distress. Skin cool, clammy. EMS administered aspirin 324mg and oxygen 4L NC en route.

VITAL SIGNS ON ARRIVAL:
BP: 168/95 mmHg
HR: 102 bpm (regular)
RR: 22 breaths/min
SpO2: 94% on 4L O2
Temp: 98.2°F

PHYSICAL EXAM:
- General: Anxious, diaphoretic, in moderate distress
- Cardiovascular: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs
- Respiratory: Mild tachypnea, clear lung sounds bilaterally
- Extremities: Cool, clammy, pulses intact

IMMEDIATE CONCERNS:
- Acute coronary syndrome (STEMI vs NSTEMI)
- Known CAD with 60% LAD stenosis
- Multiple cardiac risk factors
- Prolonged chest pain unresponsive to nitroglycerin
- Hemodynamically stable but symptomatic

triageResult.immediate_action: Perform immediate ECG and cardiac enzymes

patientHistory:
PATIENT MEDICAL RECORD
Patient ID: MRN-45892
Name: John Anderson
DOB: 03/15/1978 (Age: 45)
Gender: Male
Blood Type: O+

MEDICAL HISTORY SUMMARY:

2015 - INITIAL PRESENTATION
- Chief Complaint: Routine physical examination
- Vital Signs: BP 138/88, HR 78, Temp 98.6°F
- Diagnosed with Stage 1 Hypertension
- Prescribed: Lisinopril 10mg daily
- Lifestyle modifications recommended: diet, exercise
- Family History: Father had MI at age 62, Mother has Type 2 Diabetes

2016 - FOLLOW-UP VISITS
January 2016:
- BP improved to 128/82 on medication
- Weight: 195 lbs, BMI 28.5 (overweight)
- Lipid Panel: Total Cholesterol 245, LDL 165, HDL 38, Triglycerides 210
- Started Atorvastatin 20mg for hyperlipidemia
- Advised smoking cessation (1 pack/day for 20 years)

June 2016:
- Complained of occasional chest discomfort with exertion
- EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, no acute changes
- Stress test ordered - Result: Negative for ischemia
- Continued current medications

2017 - DIABETES DIAGNOSIS
March 2017:
- Fasting glucose: 156 mg/dL, HbA1c: 7.2%
- Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Started Metformin 500mg twice daily
- Referred to diabetes education program
- Dietary consultation completed

September 2017:
- HbA1c improved to 6.8%
- Weight: 188 lbs (lost 7 lbs)
- BP: 125/80, well controlled
- Lipids improving: LDL 142, HDL 42

2018 - CARDIAC WORKUP
February 2018:
- Presented with increased chest discomfort
- Described as pressure-like, substernal, radiating to left arm
- Occurs with moderate exertion, relieved by rest
- Duration: 5-10 minutes per episode
- Frequency: 2-3 times per week

Diagnostic Tests Performed:
- EKG: T-wave flattening in leads V4-V6
- Echocardiogram: Mild LV hypertrophy, EF 55%, no wall motion abnormalities
- Nuclear stress test: Small area of reversible ischemia in LAD territory
- Coronary angiography: 60% stenosis in mid-LAD, 40% stenosis in RCA

Treatment Plan:
- Medical management chosen over intervention
- Added Metoprolol 50mg twice daily
- Added Aspirin 81mg daily
- Added Nitroglycerin SL PRN for chest pain
- Increased Atorvastatin to 40mg
- Cardiac rehabilitation program enrollment

2019 - STABLE ANGINA MANAGEMENT
Quarterly follow-ups throughout 2019:
- Angina episodes reduced to 1-2 per month
- Medications well tolerated
- Weight: 182 lbs (continued weight loss)
- BP: 118/76, HR: 62 (on beta blocker)
- HbA1c: 6.5%, glucose control improved
- Lipids: Total 180, LDL 98, HDL 48, Triglycerides 170
- Successfully quit smoking (6 months smoke-free)

2020 - COVID-19 PANDEMIC
March 2020:
- Telehealth visits initiated due to pandemic
- Medications refilled, no changes
- Patient reports increased stress, working from home
- Exercise routine disrupted

August 2020:
- Tested positive for COVID-19
- Mild symptoms: fever, cough, fatigue
- Managed at home with supportive care
- Full recovery after 2 weeks
- No cardiac complications noted

2021 - POST-COVID MONITORING
January 2021:
- Post-COVID follow-up
- Complained of increased fatigue
- Repeat echocardiogram: EF 52%, mild decrease from baseline
- Troponin: Normal
- BNP: Mildly elevated at 125 pg/mL
- Continued current cardiac medications

June 2021:
- Fatigue improved
- Repeat echo: EF back to 55%
- Exercise tolerance improving
- Weight: 178 lbs
- BP: 120/78, HR: 64

2022 - MEDICATION ADJUSTMENTS
February 2022:
- Routine labs: Creatinine slightly elevated at 1.3 (baseline 1.0)
- eGFR: 68 mL/min (mild CKD Stage 2)
- Lisinopril dose reduced to 5mg due to kidney function
- Metformin continued (safe at this eGFR level)

July 2022:
- Complained of occasional palpitations
- 24-hour Holter monitor: Rare PVCs, no sustained arrhythmias
- Electrolytes: Normal
- Thyroid function: Normal
- Reassured, no treatment needed

November 2022:
- Annual cardiac stress test: Negative for new ischemia
- Coronary CTA: Stable CAD, no progression
- LAD stenosis unchanged at 60%
- No new lesions identified

2023 - RECENT HISTORY
January 2023:
- Routine follow-up
- All medications continued
- Weight: 175 lbs (BMI 25.5 - normal range achieved)
- BP: 122/80, HR: 66
- HbA1c: 6.3% (excellent control)
- Lipids: LDL 85, HDL 52 (at goal)

April 2023:
- Presented with chest pain episode lasting 20 minutes
- Pain resolved spontaneously before arrival
- EKG: No acute changes
- Troponin: Negative x2 (0 and 3 hours)
- Observation overnight, stress test next day: Negative
- Diagnosed as unstable angina vs GERD
- Added Omeprazole 20mg daily
- Increased Metoprolol to 75mg twice daily

August 2023:
- Doing well on current regimen
- Chest pain episodes rare (1 per month)
- Usually related to heavy meals or stress
- Responds well to nitroglycerin when needed
- Exercise tolerance good - walks 2 miles daily

CURRENT MEDICATIONS (as of December 2023):
1. Lisinopril 5mg daily (hypertension, cardioprotection)
2. Metoprolol 75mg twice daily (angina, hypertension)
3. Atorvastatin 40mg nightly (hyperlipidemia)
4. Aspirin 81mg daily (antiplatelet)
5. Metformin 500mg twice daily (diabetes)
6. Omeprazole 20mg daily (GERD)
7. Nitroglycerin 0.4mg SL PRN (angina)

ALLERGIES:
- Penicillin (rash)
- Sulfa drugs (hives)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Occupation: Software engineer (sedentary)
- Marital Status: Married, 2 children
- Alcohol: Occasional (1-2 drinks per week)
- Tobacco: Former smoker, quit 2019
- Exercise: Walks daily, light weights 3x/week
- Diet: Following cardiac diet, reduced sodium

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father: MI age 62, CABG age 65, died age 72 (heart failure)
- Mother: Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, alive age 73
- Brother: Hyperlipidemia, age 48
- Sister: Healthy, age 42

RECENT LAB VALUES (November 2023):
- CBC: WBC 7.2, Hgb 14.5, Plt 245 (normal)
- BMP: Na 140, K 4.2, Cl 102, CO2 26, BUN 18, Cr 1.2, Glucose 108
- Lipids: Total 175, LDL 85, HDL 52, Triglycerides 145
- HbA1c: 6.3%
- TSH: 2.1 (normal)
- Liver enzymes: Normal

IMAGING STUDIES ON FILE:
- Chest X-ray (2023): Clear lungs, normal cardiac silhouette
- Echocardiogram (2023): EF 55%, mild LVH, no valvular disease
- Coronary CTA (2022): Known CAD, stable
- Carotid ultrasound (2021): No significant stenosis

PROCEDURES:
- Coronary angiography (2018): 60% LAD stenosis, medical management
- Multiple stress tests (2016-2023): Most recent negative

HOSPITALIZATIONS:
- None for cardiac reasons
- COVID-19 (2020): Managed at home

EMERGENCY DEPARTMENT VISITS:
- April 2023: Chest pain, ruled out MI, discharged
- March 2018: Chest pain evaluation, admitted for cardiac cath

RISK FACTORS:
- Known coronary artery disease
- Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
- Hypertension (controlled)
- Hyperlipidemia (controlled)
- Former smoker
- Family history of premature CAD
- Mild chronic kidney disease
- Overweight (improved)

FUNCTIONAL STATUS:
- Independent in all ADLs
- Works full-time
- Exercises regularly
- No limitations in daily activities
- Occasional angina with heavy exertion

emergencyDescription:
Patient presenting to ED via EMS with acute onset severe chest pain. Pain started 45 minutes ago while shoveling snow. Described as crushing, substernal pressure 9/10 severity, radiating to left arm and jaw. Associated with profuse diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. Pain not relieved by 3 doses of home nitroglycerin. Patient appears anxious and in distress. Skin cool, clammy. EMS administered aspirin 324mg and oxygen 4L NC en route.

VITAL SIGNS ON ARRIVAL:
BP: 168/95 mmHg
HR: 102 bpm (regular)
RR: 22 breaths/min
SpO2: 94% on 4L O2
Temp: 98.2°F

PHYSICAL EXAM:
- General: Anxious, diaphoretic, in moderate distress
- Cardiovascular: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs
- Respiratory: Mild tachypnea, clear lung sounds bilaterally
- Extremities: Cool, clammy, pulses intact

IMMEDIATE CONCERNS:
- Acute coronary syndrome (STEMI vs NSTEMI)
- Known CAD with 60% LAD stenosis
- Multiple cardiac risk factors
- Prolonged chest pain unresponsive to nitroglycerin
- Hemodynamically stable but symptomatic

triageResult.immediate_action: Perform immediate ECG and cardiac enzymes
